IDirect3DDevice8::LoadVertexShaderProgram

Loads a vertex shader into a section of the 136 available addresses on the GPU.

HRESULT LoadVertexShaderProgram(
  CONST DWORD *pFunction,
  DWORD Address
);

Parameters

pFunction
[in] Pointer to the vertex shader function token array. This parameter defines the operations to apply to each vertex. Cannot be NULL.
Address
[in] The location on the GPU to insert the vertex shader, in the range 0 through 135.

Return Values

If the method succeeds, the return value is D3D_OK.

If the method fails, the return value can be D3DERR_INVALIDCALL.

Remarks

LoadVertexShaderProgam is new and acts like IDirect3DDevice8::LoadVertexShader, except that it takes the IDirect3DDevice8::CreateVertexShader program declaration directly. In other words, it allows specific control over the vertex shader program registers, without forcing Direct3D to do a copy of the program, and allowing partial program updates (such as when loading program sub-modules).

The GPU used by the Xbox supports the loading of multiple vertex shaders into a set of 136 available addresses on the chip. Note, however, that the calling application must keep track of the allocation of addresses per vertex shader. No error checking for overwriting a previously allocated set of addresses is provided.

IDirect3DDevice8::SelectVertexShader is used to select which of the vertex shader programs loaded by LoadVertexShaderProgram to use as the current vertex shader program.

In general, a subsequent call to IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShader will invalidate any vertex shaders loaded by LoadVertexShader, and require them to be reloaded. However, if SetVertexShader is called using D3DFVF_XYZRHW, only instruction slots 0 through D3DVS_XBOX_RESERVEDXYZRHWSLOTS – 1 will be destroyed. This means that if a title is using programmable vertex shaders in conjunction with fixed-function vertex shaders, it is recommended that the first programmable vertex shader be loaded, beginning at address D3DVS_XBOX_RESERVEDXYZRHWSLOTS, and each subsesquent programmable shader should be added at its end.

Through careful experimentation, it is possible to discover which instruction slots are invalidated by the fixed function pipeline and therefore load vertex shaders into addresses that will not be invalidated through the use of the fixed-function pipeline.

The size of a vertex shader can determined from the second WORD in the vertex shader token array. If the first parameter to LoadVertexShaderProgram is pFunction, then the size of the vertex shader would be:

size = *pFunction >> 16;

Requirements

  Header: Declared in D3d8.h.
  Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::GetVertexShaderSize, IDirect3DDevice8::GetShaderConstantMode, IDirect3DDevice8::LoadVertexShader, IDirect3DDevice8::SelectVertexShader, IDirect3DDevice8::RunVertexStateShader, IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShader
